clans.name: MacDonald
clans.motto: Per mare per terras (By sea and by land)
clans.tartan: Red field with green and blue stripes, crossed with thin white and yellow lines
clans.history: The MacDonalds are one of the largest and most famous Highland clans, claiming descent from Somerled, Lord of the Isles. They once ruled vast territories in the Western Highlands and Islands, earning the title 'Lords of the Isles'. The clan played crucial roles in Scottish history, from supporting Robert the Bruce to the Jacobite uprisings.
famous_members: Flora MacDonald (helped Bonnie Prince Charlie escape)
John MacDonald, 1st Lord of the Isles
clans.traditional_recipe: Cullen Skink - Smoked haddock soup with potatoes and onions, a warming dish from the coastal regions

clans.name: Campbell
clans.tartan: Dark blue field with green stripes, crossed with thin black and white lines
clans.history: The Campbells rose to become one of the most powerful Highland clans, often serving as agents of the Scottish crown. Based in Argyll, they expanded their influence through strategic marriages and royal favor. The clan is infamous for the Massacre of Glencoe in 1692, where they led government forces against the MacDonalds.
clans.traditional_recipe: Stovies - Potato dish with onions and leftover meat, cooked slowly in one pot

clans.name: Stewart
clans.motto: Virescit vulnere virtus (Courage grows strong at a wound)
clans.tartan: Red field with green, blue and white stripes in various patterns
clans.history: The Royal House of Stewart ruled Scotland for over 300 years and later the United Kingdom. Originating as hereditary stewards to the kings of Scotland, they rose to the throne through marriage. The clan includes many branches throughout Scotland, all claiming descent from the royal line.
clans.traditional_recipe: Cock-a-leekie Soup - Chicken and leek soup with prunes, traditionally served at royal occasions

clans.name: MacLeod
clans.tartan: Yellow field with black stripes and red accents
clans.history: The MacLeods are one of the principal clans of the Isle of Skye and the Outer Hebrides. They claim Norse ancestry and have held lands in the Hebrides for over 700 years. The clan is divided into two main branches: MacLeods of Dunvegan (Skye) and MacLeods of Lewis, both with rich maritime traditions.
clans.traditional_recipe: Hebridean Fish Stew - Fresh fish with tatties and neeps, seasoned with sea salt and herbs

clans.name: Fraser
clans.tartan: Red field with green and blue stripes, distinctive white line pattern
clans.history: The Frasers are a Highland clan with both Norman and Gaelic roots. They established themselves in the Scottish Highlands and became powerful landowners. The clan fought at Culloden in 1746 under Simon Fraser, and their war cry 'Caisteal Dhuni' echoed across many battlefields.
clans.traditional_recipe: Venison Collops - Thin slices of venison cooked with oatmeal and wild herbs

clans.name: Gordon
clans.tartan: Dark green field with navy blue and black stripes, yellow accents
clans.history: The Gordons became one of the most powerful families in the north-east of Scotland. They were granted extensive lands in Aberdeenshire and became Earls of Huntly. The clan played significant roles in Scottish politics and military affairs, often serving as the king's representatives in the north.
clans.traditional_recipe: Aberdeen Angus Beef Stew - Slow-cooked beef with root vegetables and barley

clans.name: Murray
clans.tartan: Blue field with green stripes and red accents
clans.history: The Murrays of Atholl became one of the most influential Highland clans, with their seat at Blair Castle. They played crucial roles in Scottish history, from the Wars of Independence to the Jacobite period. The clan motto reflects their readiness for battle and service to Scotland.
clans.traditional_recipe: Atholl Brose - Traditional drink made with whisky, honey, oatmeal and cream

clans.name: Cameron
clans.tartan: Red, green and blue stripes with yellow accents
clans.history: The Camerons are a West Highland clan known for their fierce independence and warrior tradition. Based in Lochaber, they were among the most loyal supporters of the Jacobite cause. The clan's history is filled with tales of bravery, feuds, and unwavering loyalty to their chiefs.
clans.traditional_recipe: Highland Bannocks - Oatcakes cooked on a griddle, perfect with heather honey

clans.name: Ross
clans.tartan: Red field with green and blue checks
clans.history: The Rosses were powerful in the northern Highlands, with their stronghold at Balnagowan Castle. They claim descent from Fearchar Mac an t-Sagairt, Earl of Ross. The clan controlled vast territories and played important roles in Highland politics and warfare.
clans.traditional_recipe: Finnan Haddie - Smoked haddock traditionally prepared in the coastal regions

clans.name: MacKenzie
clans.motto: Luceo non uro (I shine, not burn)
clans.tartan: Blue and green with red and black stripes
clans.history: The MacKenzies rose from obscurity to become Earls of Seaforth and one of the most powerful Highland clans. They controlled vast territories in the north-west Highlands and were known for their military prowess and political acumen. The clan's fortune was often guided by the prophecies of the Brahan Seer.
clans.traditional_recipe: Crowdie - Fresh Highland cheese served with oatcakes and wild berries

clans.name: Armstrong
clans.tartan: Blue field with green and red stripes
clans.history: The Armstrongs were one of the most notorious Border Reiver families, controlling the Debatable Lands between Scotland and England. Known for their fierce independence and cattle raiding, they were feared by both Scottish and English authorities. Their stronghold was Gilnockie Tower in Liddesdale.
clans.traditional_recipe: Border Tart - Rich pastry filled with dried fruits and spices, perfect for long winter nights

clans.name: Graham
clans.tartan: Red and green with blue and white stripes
clans.history: The Grahams trace their origins to William de Graham who came to Scotland in the 12th century. They became powerful in central Scotland and produced notable military leaders. The clan is forever linked to the heroic James Graham, 1st Marquess of Montrose.
clans.traditional_recipe: Graham Griddle Cakes - Sweet oatcakes cooked on a hot griddle

clans.name: Sinclair
clans.tartan: Green field with blue and red stripes, white accents
clans.history: The Sinclairs came to Scotland from Normandy and became Earls of Orkney and Caithness. They built the magnificent Rosslyn Chapel and were involved in the Knights Templar. The clan controlled vast northern territories and maintained strong Norse connections.
clans.traditional_recipe: Orkney Oatcakes - Thin, crispy oatcakes perfect with cheese and crowdie

clans.name: Bruce
clans.tartan: Red field with green stripes and gold accents
clans.history: The Bruces are forever associated with Robert the Bruce, King of Scots and hero of Scottish independence. The family came from Normandy and established themselves as powerful nobles. Their legacy is crowned by Robert I's victory at Bannockburn in 1314.
famous_members: Robert the Bruce, King of Scots
Edward Bruce, High King of Ireland
clans.traditional_recipe: King Robert's Bannocks - Hearty oat and barley cakes that sustained armies

clans.name: Douglas
clans.tartan: Dark green field with blue stripes and red accents
clans.history: The Douglas clan produced some of Scotland's greatest warriors, earning the name 'Black Douglases' for their fierce reputation in battle. Sir James Douglas was Robert the Bruce's most trusted lieutenant, earning the nickname 'Good Sir James' from allies and 'Black Douglas' from enemies.
clans.traditional_recipe: Douglas Lamb Stew - Slow-cooked lamb with root vegetables and herbs

clans.name: Cunningham
clans.tartan: Green field with blue and red stripes
clans.history: The Cunninghams were granted lands in Ayrshire and became powerful in the west of Scotland. The clan was involved in many feuds, particularly with the Montgomeries. They held the hereditary office of Justiciars of Carrick.
clans.traditional_recipe: Ayrshire Bacon and Egg Pie - Traditional savory pie from their heartland

clans.name: Keith
clans.tartan: Red field with green stripes and white lines
clans.history: The Keiths held the hereditary office of Great Marischal of Scotland for over 600 years. Based in the northeast, they were powerful nobles who built Dunnottar Castle. The clan played important roles in Scottish military history.
clans.traditional_recipe: Marischal's Beef - Slow-roasted beef with neep and tattie accompaniment

clans.name: Leslie
clans.tartan: Blue and green with red and white stripes
clans.history: The Leslies came to Scotland from Hungary and became prominent in the northeast. They produced skilled military commanders who served across Europe. The clan held lands in both Aberdeenshire and Fife.
clans.traditional_recipe: Leslie's Shortbread - Rich buttery shortbread, a family tradition

clans.name: Hay
clans.tartan: Red field with green and blue checks
clans.history: The Hays became Earls of Erroll and held the hereditary office of Lord High Constable of Scotland. They were granted lands for their service at the Battle of Luncarty. The family built several notable castles including Delgatie and Slains.
famous_members: Sir Gilbert Hay, 1st Earl of Erroll
clans.traditional_recipe: Erroll's Game Pie - Rich pie filled with venison and game birds

clans.name: Menzies
clans.motto: Vil God I zal (Will God, I shall)
clans.tartan: Red and white checks with green and blue accents
clans.history: The Menzies (pronounced Ming-iss) were granted lands in Perthshire and became chiefs of Glen Lyon. They supported Robert the Bruce and were rewarded with extensive territories. The clan castle at Castle Menzies was their seat for centuries.
clans.traditional_recipe: Glen Lyon Trout - Fresh trout from highland streams, simply prepared

clans.name: Scott
clans.tartan: Red field with green stripes and blue accents
clans.history: The Scotts were one of the great Border families, controlling much of the Scottish Borders. They were skilled horsemen and warriors, involved in constant Border warfare. The clan produced the famous writer Sir Walter Scott.
clans.traditional_recipe: Border Shortbread - Rich, crumbly shortbread from the Borders tradition

clans.name: Kennedy
clans.tartan: Blue field with green and red stripes
clans.history: The Kennedys were chiefs of Carrick in Ayrshire and held the title of Earls of Cassillis. They were a powerful west coast clan with strong political connections. The family name means 'ugly head' in Gaelic, possibly referring to a helmeted warrior.
clans.traditional_recipe: Carrick Clootie Dumpling - Steamed pudding with dried fruits and spices

clans.name: Robertson
clans.motto: Virtutis gloria merces (Glory is the reward of valor)
clans.tartan: Red field with green and blue stripes, hunting version in darker tones
clans.history: The Robertsons are also known as Clan Donnachaidh, claiming descent from Duncan, friend of Robert the Bruce. Based in Rannoch and Atholl, they were fierce Highland warriors. The clan name Robertson comes from 'Robert's sons', referring to Robert the Bruce.
clans.traditional_recipe: Struan Micheal - Traditional harvest cake blessed on Michaelmas

clans.name: Maclean
clans.tartan: Blue field with green and black stripes
clans.history: The Macleans were one of the most powerful clans of the Western Isles, controlling Mull, Tiree, and parts of the mainland. They were fierce warriors known for their loyalty and bravery. The clan motto reflects their warrior spirit and code of honor.
clans.traditional_recipe: Isle of Mull Cheddar Tart - Cheese tart using the famous island cheddar

clans.name: MacPherson
clans.motto: Touch not the cat bot a glove
clans.tartan: Green field with red and blue stripes, white lines
clans.history: The MacPhersons were the leading clan of Clan Chattan confederation. Based in Badenoch, they were known as fierce warriors and skilled musicians. The clan motto refers to the wildcat, their heraldic symbol, warning not to touch without proper protection.
clans.traditional_recipe: Badenoch Brose - Oatmeal dish with butter and honey, sometimes with whisky

clans.name: Grant
clans.tartan: Red field with green and blue stripes
clans.history: The Grants settled in Strathspey and became one of the most powerful Highland clans. They were known for their independence and military prowess. The clan expanded their territory through strategic marriages and warfare, eventually controlling much of Strathspey.
clans.traditional_recipe: Strathspey Venison - Roasted venison with juniper berries and herbs

clans.name: Sutherland
clans.tartan: Green field with red and blue stripes, yellow accents
clans.history: The Sutherlands were one of the most powerful clans in the far north of Scotland. The clan name comes from the Norse 'Sudrland' meaning southern land. They built Dunrobin Castle and controlled vast territories in the Highlands.
clans.traditional_recipe: Highland Salmon - Fresh salmon prepared with wild herbs from the Highlands

clans.name: Munro
clans.tartan: Red field with green and blue stripes
clans.history: The Munros were chiefs in Easter Ross, claiming descent from Irish settlers. They were staunch Protestants and supporters of the Government. The clan is famous for giving their name to 'Munros' - Scottish mountains over 3,000 feet, named after Sir Hugh Munro who catalogued them.
clans.traditional_recipe: Easter Ross Barley Broth - Hearty soup with barley, vegetables and lamb

clans.name: Urquhart
clans.motto: Meane weil, speak weil, and doe weil
clans.tartan: Yellow field with red and green stripes
clans.history: The Urquharts held lands in Moray and later in Cromarty. They were hereditary sheriffs of Cromarty for over 400 years. The clan castle at Urquhart on Loch Ness was one of the largest castles in Scotland before its destruction.
clans.traditional_recipe: Cromarty Firth Fish Pie - Traditional fish pie from their coastal territories

clans.name: MacNeil
clans.motto: Vincere vel mori (To conquer or die)
clans.tartan: Yellow field with black stripes and red accents
clans.history: The MacNeils were sea-lords of Barra in the Outer Hebrides, claiming descent from Niall of the Nine Hostages, High King of Ireland. They were known for their seamanship and independence, often acting as pirates and raiders from their island stronghold.
clans.traditional_recipe: Barra Bannocks - Island oatcakes perfect with fresh fish
